questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What must you do at a steady red traffic light?
choices: Slow down and proceed if clear
Stop only if other cars are present
Yield to traffic on your left
questions.explanation: At a red light you must come to a complete stop at the stop line, or before the crosswalk or intersection if there is no stop line.

questions.prompt: A steady yellow traffic light means:
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn red; stop if you can do so safely
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that the signal is about to change to red. Stop if you can do so safely; do not speed up to beat the light.

questions.prompt: When may you turn right on a red light in Virginia?
choices: After coming to a complete stop and yielding, unless a sign prohibits it
Only if no sign is posted, without stopping
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after a complete stop and yielding to traffic and pedestrians, unless a 'No Turn on Red' sign is posted.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal means:
choices: Stop, then proceed when safe, as at a stop sign
The signal is broken; ignore it
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means the same as a stop sign: come to a complete stop, then proceed when it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic signal means:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means slow down and proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: When traffic signals are completely dark because of a power outage, you should:
choices: Treat the intersection as an all-way stop
Wait for the lights to come back on
questions.explanation: When signals are not displaying any lights, you must stop and proceed as though the intersection were an all-way stop.

questions.prompt: A green arrow signal means:
choices: You may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear
Yield to all traffic before turning
Pedestrians have the right of way only
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives a protected movement: you may proceed in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: You want to turn left at a green light with no green arrow. You must:
choices: Turn immediately, you have the right of way
Yield to oncoming traffic and pedestrians, then turn when clear
Stop and wait for a green arrow
questions.explanation: On a solid green light, left-turning traffic must yield to oncoming vehicles and pedestrians before completing the turn.

questions.prompt: An eight-sided (octagon) sign always means:
questions.explanation: The octagon shape is reserved exclusively for stop signs and always means come to a complete stop.

questions.prompt: A red circle with a slash through a symbol means:
choices: NO — the action shown is prohibited
questions.explanation: A red circle with a slash means NO: the symbol shows an action that is not allowed.

questions.prompt: A yellow, diamond-shaped sign is used to:
choices: Give a regulation you must obey
Warn of conditions or hazards ahead
questions.explanation: Diamond-shaped warning signs alert you to special conditions or hazards ahead — slow down and be ready to react.

questions.prompt: Broken yellow center lines on a two-lane road mean:
choices: Passing on the left is allowed in either direction when clear
questions.explanation: Yellow lines separate opposing traffic. A broken yellow center line means passing on the left is allowed in either direction when the way ahead is clear.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line next to a broken yellow line means:
choices: Passing is allowed only from the broken-line side
questions.explanation: Passing is allowed from the side of the broken line, but not from the side of the solid yellow line.

questions.prompt: White lines on the road separate:
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
questions.explanation: Broken white lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction; you may change lanes with caution.

questions.prompt: A five-sided (pentagon) sign indicates:
choices: A school zone or school crossing
questions.explanation: The pentagon shape marks school zones and warns of school crossings.

questions.prompt: A round (circular) sign is most often used for:
questions.explanation: The round shape is used for railroad advance-warning signs.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop where two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach an intersection at the same time, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When approaching a roundabout, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout and enter when there is a gap
Come to a stop in the circle to let others in
questions.explanation: Traffic in a roundabout has the right of way. Yield to vehicles already circulating and enter when there is a safe gap.

questions.prompt: You must yield the right-of-way to:
choices: Pedestrians in a crosswalk and emergency vehicles using lights/sirens
No one if you have a green light
questions.explanation: You must yield to pedestrians in crosswalks and to emergency vehicles displaying flashing lights and/or sirens.

questions.prompt: When you approach an emergency vehicle stopped on the roadside with lights flashing, Virginia law says you should:
choices: Move over to another lane, or slow down if you cannot move over
questions.explanation: When approaching a stationary emergency vehicle or tow truck with flashing lights, change to a lane not next to it if you can do so safely; if you cannot change lanes, reduce your speed and proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: A driver entering a road from a driveway or private road must:
choices: Yield to vehicles already on the road
Stop all traffic on the road
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering or crossing a roadway from a driveway or private road must yield to traffic already on the road.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with a yield sign, you should:
choices: Always come to a full stop
Slow down, be ready to stop, and give the right-of-way to traffic and pedestrians
questions.explanation: A yield sign means slow down, be prepared to stop, and let cross traffic and pedestrians go before you proceed.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in a Virginia school, business, or residential area is:
questions.explanation: Unless a sign states otherwise, the maximum speed for passenger vehicles in school, business and residential areas is 25 MPH.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the maximum speed limit on most other (non-residential, paved) Virginia roads is:
questions.explanation: Unless otherwise posted, the maximum speed limit is 55 MPH for all roads other than residential/business/school areas (25) and unpaved roads (35).

questions.prompt: You can be charged with reckless driving in Virginia if you drive:
choices: 20 or more MPH above the limit, or over 85 MPH
Only if you cause a crash
questions.explanation: Driving 20 or more MPH above the speed limit, or faster than 85 MPH, can bring a reckless driving charge regardless of the posted limit.

questions.prompt: A 'speed limit' is best described as:
choices: The slowest you may legally drive
The maximum legal speed under ideal conditions
The speed you must drive at all times
questions.explanation: A speed limit is the maximum legal speed under ideal conditions. You may drive slower, and you must drive slower when conditions are poor.

questions.prompt: The safe following distance for speeds under 35 MPH is at least:
questions.explanation: Use a 2-second following distance under 35 MPH, 3 seconds at 35-45 MPH, and 4 seconds at 46-70 MPH.

questions.prompt: On wet or slippery roads, you should increase your following distance because:
questions.explanation: Wet, icy or slippery surfaces increase stopping distance, so you need a larger space cushion to stop safely.

questions.prompt: The three factors that determine total stopping distance are:
choices: Perception time, reaction distance, and braking distance
questions.explanation: Total stopping distance is the sum of perception time, reaction distance, and braking distance.

questions.prompt: Using a radar detector in Virginia is:
questions.explanation: It is illegal to use radar detectors in Virginia.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and check your blind spot over your shoulder
questions.explanation: Signal your intention, check your mirrors, and glance over your shoulder to check the blind spot before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: When passing another vehicle, it is legal to:
choices: Exceed the speed limit to pass quickly
Complete the pass before a no-passing zone and without exceeding the speed limit
Pass on a hill or curve
questions.explanation: You must not exceed the speed limit while passing, and you must complete the pass before reaching a no-passing zone.

questions.prompt: In Virginia, a U-turn is treated as:
choices: Two left turns, and is illegal where a 'No Left Turn' sign is posted
questions.explanation: U-turns are considered two left turns and are illegal where a No Left Turn sign is posted.

questions.prompt: To make a right turn, you should generally be in:
choices: The lane furthest to the right
questions.explanation: Position your vehicle in the lane furthest to the right before making a right turn.

questions.prompt: When should you signal before turning or changing lanes?
choices: Well in advance, to warn other drivers
Only if other cars are near
questions.explanation: Signal in advance so other drivers and pedestrians know your intentions before you begin the maneuver.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Steer in the direction you want the vehicle to go and ease off the gas
Turn the wheel sharply the opposite way
questions.explanation: Ease off the accelerator and steer in the direction you want the front of the vehicle to go; avoid braking hard, which can worsen a skid.

questions.prompt: When you must stop for a stopped school bus with flashing red lights and an extended stop arm, you must remain stopped until:
choices: All children are clear of the road and the bus moves again
questions.explanation: Stay stopped until all children are clear of the roadway and the bus begins to move again.

questions.prompt: On an undivided road, when a school bus stops with red lights flashing, which vehicles must stop?
choices: Only vehicles in the same lane
questions.explanation: On an undivided roadway, vehicles approaching from any direction must stop for a stopped school bus with flashing red lights.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Follow closely so it can see you
questions.explanation: Allow extra following distance behind a motorcycle, since motorcycles can stop quickly and are harder to see.

questions.prompt: Before turning, changing lanes, or opening your door, you should always check for:
choices: Nothing if you have a green light
questions.explanation: Always check for bicyclists and motorcyclists — they are smaller and easy to miss — before pulling out, turning, changing lanes, or opening a door.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have big blind spots, so you should:
choices: Avoid lingering beside or directly behind them
questions.explanation: Trucks have large blind spots (No-Zones). Avoid lingering beside or right behind a truck; if you can't see the driver's mirrors, they can't see you.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian with a white cane or guide dog at a crossing, you must:
choices: Yield and let them cross safely
questions.explanation: Pedestrians using a white cane or guide dog have the right of way; yield and allow them to cross safely.

questions.prompt: You must use your headlights:
choices: From sunset to sunrise and whenever you use your windshield wipers in bad weather
Only when other cars are present
questions.explanation: Use headlights from sunset to sunrise and any time conditions (rain, snow, fog) require your wipers or reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: In heavy fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: Use low-beam headlights in fog. High beams reflect off the fog and glare back, reducing your visibility.

questions.prompt: You should dim your high beams to low when an oncoming vehicle is within:
choices: You never need to dim them
questions.explanation: Dim your high beams to low beams when within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle, and when following within 200 feet of the vehicle ahead, to avoid blinding other drivers.

questions.prompt: To reduce glare from the headlights of an oncoming vehicle at night, you should look:
choices: Toward the right edge of your lane
questions.explanation: Look toward the right edge of the road or your lane to avoid being blinded by oncoming headlights.

questions.prompt: Properly adjusted side mirrors should let you:
choices: Barely see the sides of your own vehicle to reduce blind spots
questions.explanation: Adjust side mirrors so you can just barely see the sides of your vehicle; this widens your view and reduces blind spots.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle starts to hydroplane on a wet road, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and avoid sudden steering or braking
questions.explanation: If you hydroplane, ease off the accelerator and keep the steering steady; sudden braking or steering can cause a skid.

questions.prompt: When driving in rain, snow, or ice, you should:
choices: Drive at the posted limit regardless
Reduce your speed and increase following distance
questions.explanation: In poor conditions, slow down below the posted limit if needed and increase your following distance to allow for longer stopping.

questions.prompt: If you see a deer crossing sign and it is dusk, you should:
choices: Speed up to pass the area quickly
Slow down and stay alert, especially at dawn and dusk
Ignore it unless you see a deer
questions.explanation: Deer are most active at dawn and dusk. Slow down, stay alert, and be ready to stop where deer crossing signs are posted.

questions.prompt: If a deer or large animal is suddenly in your path and you cannot stop, you should:
choices: Brake firmly and stay in your lane rather than swerving
questions.explanation: Brake firmly and try to stay in your lane. Swerving can cause a more serious crash with another vehicle or a fixed object.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle's wheels drift off onto the road shoulder, you should:
choices: Brake hard and jerk the wheel back
Ease off the gas, slow down, then steer back gently when it's safe
questions.explanation: Don't overcorrect. Ease off the accelerator, slow down, and steer gently back onto the roadway when it is safe.

questions.prompt: When driving in heavy rain, your tires can lose contact with the road. This is called:
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when a layer of water gets between your tires and the road, causing a loss of traction.

questions.prompt: For a driver age 21 or older, the blood alcohol concentration (BAC) at which they are considered to be driving under the influence is:
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are considered to be driving under the influence (DUI) at a BAC of .08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Virginia's zero-tolerance law, a driver under age 21 can be charged with illegal alcohol consumption at a BAC of:
choices: At least .02 but less than .08 percent
questions.explanation: If you are under 21, a BAC of at least .02 but less than .08 can result in a conviction for illegal consumption of alcohol.

questions.prompt: Drinking alcohol affects your driving by:
questions.explanation: Alcohol impairs your coordination, vision, and judgment — all critical to safe driving — even at low levels.

questions.prompt: The only thing that will reliably sober up a person who has been drinking is:
questions.explanation: Only time allows your body to eliminate alcohol. Coffee, showers, and fresh air do not lower your BAC.

questions.prompt: Texting or using a handheld phone while driving is dangerous because it causes:
choices: Visual, manual, and mental distraction at once
questions.explanation: Texting takes your eyes off the road, your hands off the wheel, and your mind off driving — visual, manual, and cognitive distraction together.

questions.prompt: If you become drowsy while driving, the safest action is to:
choices: Open the window and keep going
Stop and rest, or change drivers
questions.explanation: The only safe cure for drowsiness is to stop driving and rest or let someone else drive. Drowsy driving slows reactions like alcohol does.

questions.prompt: Aggressive driving behaviors such as tailgating and weaving through traffic:
choices: Save significant time and are legal
Increase the risk of a crash and can bring penalties
Are fine if you are in a hurry
Are only a problem at night
questions.explanation: Aggressive driving greatly raises crash risk and can result in reckless or aggressive driving charges and license suspension.

questions.prompt: If you refuse a breath test or have a BAC of .08 or higher when charged with DUI, your license is automatically suspended for a first offense for:
questions.explanation: An administrative license suspension of 7 days applies to a first DUI offense when you refuse the breath test or register .08 or higher.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you must:
choices: Leave if no police are present
Stop, help the injured, and exchange information
Move the cars and forget it
questions.explanation: You must stop at the scene, render aid to anyone injured, and exchange information. Leaving the scene is a serious offense.

questions.prompt: When a law enforcement officer signals you to pull over, you should:
choices: Stop in the travel lane immediately
Pull over to the right safely, stop, and keep your hands in view
Speed up to find a police station
Get out and walk to the officer
questions.explanation: Pull over safely to the right, stop, stay in the vehicle, and keep your hands in plain view, preferably on the wheel.

questions.prompt: During a traffic stop, you should keep your seat belt fastened and:
choices: Keep your hands in plain view and follow the officer's instructions
Reach into the glove box right away
questions.explanation: Stay buckled, keep your hands visible, and follow the officer's directions. Reach for documents only when asked.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle stalls on railroad tracks and a train is coming, you should:
choices: Stay in the car and try to restart it
Get everyone out and away from the tracks immediately
Wait for help in the car
questions.explanation: Get yourself and all passengers out and far away from the tracks at once; do not try to restart or move the vehicle.

questions.prompt: If you approach a railroad crossing with a gate that is down and lights flashing, you must:
choices: Drive around the gate if no train is visible
Stop and wait until the gate rises and lights stop
questions.explanation: Never drive around a lowered gate. Stop and wait until the gates are fully raised and the lights stop flashing.

questions.prompt: Under Virginia law, who must wear a safety belt?
choices: The driver and all passengers, front and rear
questions.explanation: Virginia law requires the driver and all passengers, in both front and rear seats, to wear safety belts.

questions.prompt: A driver transporting anyone younger than age 18 must:
choices: Ensure each is secured in a belt, booster, or child seat, wherever seated
Let them choose whether to buckle
questions.explanation: A driver carrying anyone under 18 must make sure each is properly secured in a safety belt, booster, or child safety seat, no matter where they sit.

questions.prompt: In Virginia, children under what age must be secured in a child safety seat or booster seat?
questions.explanation: All children under age 8 must be properly secured in an approved child safety seat or booster seat.

questions.prompt: A rear-facing child safety seat should never be placed:
choices: In front of an active passenger-side air bag
questions.explanation: Never put a rear-facing child seat in front of an active passenger air bag; the deploying bag can seriously injure the child.

questions.prompt: Children should ride rear-facing:
choices: From birth to at least age 2, or as long as the seat allows
questions.explanation: Children should ride rear-facing from birth to age 2, or as long as the safety-seat manufacturer allows.

questions.prompt: The safest place to install a child safety seat is:
choices: The center of the back seat
questions.explanation: The center of the back seat is the safest position for a properly installed child safety seat.

questions.prompt: Air bags work best when:
choices: The seat is pushed all the way forward
You sit very close to the wheel
questions.explanation: Air bags are designed to work with safety belts, not replace them. Always wear your belt and sit back from the wheel.

questions.prompt: Transporting a child under age 16 in the open bed of a pickup truck in Virginia is:
choices: Legal if they wear a belt
questions.explanation: It is illegal to transport children under age 16 in the bed of a pickup truck, even with a camper shell.

questions.prompt: A Virginia learner's permit allows you to drive only when:
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 (or qualifying relative 18-20) is in the front passenger seat
questions.explanation: With a learner's permit you may drive only when a licensed driver age 21+ — or a qualifying relative who is 18-20 — is seated in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: Whenever you drive, Virginia law requires you to:
choices: Carry a valid driver's license or learner's permit
questions.explanation: You must carry a valid driver's license or learner's permit whenever you drive; misuse of either is illegal.

questions.prompt: DMV assigns demerit points to convictions of:
choices: 3, 4, or 6 points depending on the offense
questions.explanation: Convictions are assigned three, four, or six demerit points depending on the seriousness of the violation.

questions.prompt: For a driver under 18, a first conviction of a demerit-point violation results in:
questions.explanation: A first demerit-point (or safety belt/child restraint) conviction committed under age 18 requires completion of a driver improvement clinic.

questions.prompt: If your driver's license is suspended, it means your driving privilege is:
choices: Withdrawn temporarily and can be reinstated
questions.explanation: A suspension withdraws your driving privilege temporarily; you may reinstate it after the suspension period by meeting requirements and paying fees.

questions.prompt: If you fail the knowledge exam and are under age 18, you must wait before retaking it:
questions.explanation: By law, applicants under 18 who fail the knowledge exam must wait a full 15 days before retaking it.

questions.prompt: An adult driver who accumulates 12 demerit points in 12 months will be required to:
questions.explanation: Drivers 18 or older who accumulate 12 demerit points in 12 months (or 18 in 24 months) must complete a driver improvement clinic.

questions.prompt: Inside a roundabout, traffic must travel:
questions.explanation: After entering a roundabout, drivers must travel in a counter-clockwise direction around the center island.

questions.prompt: When entering an interstate highway from an entrance ramp, you must:
choices: Stop at the end of the ramp
Yield to traffic already on the highway
Expect highway traffic to yield to you
questions.explanation: Drivers entering an interstate from an entrance ramp must yield the right-of-way to traffic already on the highway.

questions.prompt: When you meet a funeral procession, you should:
choices: Cut through it to save time
Not cut through, join, or interfere with it
Force the lead car to stop
questions.explanation: You must yield to funeral processions; do not cut through, join, or interfere with a funeral procession.

questions.prompt: An emergency vehicle approaches you from behind using lights and siren. You should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull over to the right edge of the road and stop until it passes
questions.explanation: You must immediately yield, pull over safely to the right edge of the road, and stop until the emergency vehicle has passed.

questions.prompt: You must come to a full stop and yield for a pedestrian who is:
choices: Lawfully crossing the roadway in a crosswalk
questions.explanation: Drivers are required to come to a full stop for pedestrians who are lawfully crossing a roadway; failing to do so can be a Class 1 misdemeanor if it causes serious injury or death.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist, Virginia law requires you to pass at least:
questions.explanation: State law requires motorists to pass a bicyclist with at least three feet of clearance; if the lane is too narrow, change lanes to pass safely.

questions.prompt: You may turn left at a red light only when:
choices: Turning from a one-way street onto another one-way street, after stopping
Any time the way is clear
questions.explanation: You may turn left on red only when turning from a one-way street onto another one-way street, after a complete stop, unless a sign or red arrow prohibits it.

questions.prompt: Passing on the right is permitted when:
choices: The vehicle ahead has signaled and is making a left turn
You must drive onto the shoulder to get around
questions.explanation: You may pass on the right when the vehicle ahead has signaled and is making a left turn, but not if you must leave the pavement to do so.

questions.prompt: How far ahead of a turn should you signal your intention?
choices: About 100 feet (three to four seconds) before the turn
questions.explanation: You should signal at least three to four seconds, or about 100 feet, ahead of your turn.

questions.prompt: In business districts, cities, and towns, U-turns are allowed:
questions.explanation: In business districts, cities, and towns, U-turns are allowed only at intersections. Never make a U-turn on a highway.

questions.prompt: Passing is unlawful and unsafe:
choices: On hills, curves, at intersections, or railroad crossings (unless multiple same-direction lanes)
questions.explanation: Passing is unlawful and unsafe on hills, curves, at intersections or railroad crossings, except on roads with two or more lanes in the same direction.

questions.prompt: You do NOT have to stop for a stopped school bus with flashing red lights when:
choices: You are on the opposite side of a divided road separated by a median or barrier
The bus is on a two-lane road
questions.explanation: You do not have to stop if you are traveling in the opposite direction on a roadway divided by a median or barrier and the bus is on the other side.

questions.prompt: Bicyclists are legally allowed to ride on:
choices: All public roads except interstates and most freeways
questions.explanation: Bicyclists are legally allowed on all public roads except interstates and most freeways (limited access highways).

questions.prompt: More than half of fatal motorcycle crashes involving cars happen because:
choices: The other driver did not see the motorcycle
questions.explanation: In more than half of all crashes involving motorcycles and automobiles, the other driver did not see the motorcycle in time. Look twice for motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: A slow-moving vehicle emblem (orange triangle) means the vehicle travels at:
questions.explanation: Slow-moving vehicles such as farm equipment or horse-drawn vehicles travel at 25 MPH or less and must display the slow-moving vehicle emblem on public highways.

questions.prompt: When you want to drive next to a motorcycle in the same lane, you should:
choices: Do it, since motorcycles are narrow
Never drive beside a motorcycle in the same lane
questions.explanation: Never drive beside a motorcycle in the same lane. Although narrower than the lane, a rider needs and may use the full width of the lane.

questions.prompt: A bicycle lane marked on the roadway may be driven in by a motor vehicle:
choices: Only when necessary to turn left or right
questions.explanation: Drivers should not drive in a bicycle lane except when necessary to turn left or right, and must yield to bicycles in the lane.

questions.prompt: Bridges and overpasses in cold weather are especially dangerous because:
choices: They freeze before other road surfaces
questions.explanation: Watch for ice on bridges and in shady areas; bridges freeze before other road surfaces.

questions.prompt: When rain first begins, roads are most slippery during the first:
questions.explanation: During the first half-hour of rain, roads are more likely to be slippery because oil on the road surface mixes with the water.

questions.prompt: When you need to stop on a slippery or snowy surface, you should:
choices: Release the accelerator and apply the brakes gently
questions.explanation: On slippery surfaces, release the accelerator and apply the brakes gently; you have the most traction and control when the front tires are rolling.

questions.prompt: In hazardous conditions where visibility is reduced, your first response should be to:
choices: Speed up to clear the area
questions.explanation: Reducing your speed should be your first response to decreased visibility and dangerous road conditions; also increase your following distance and turn on your headlights.

questions.prompt: In hazardous weather, you should increase your normal following distance by:
questions.explanation: Increase your space cushion by doubling your normal following distance when driving in hazardous conditions.

questions.prompt: Before driving after a snowfall, you should:
choices: Remove snow and ice from the entire car, including roof, windows, mirrors, and lights
Leave snow on the roof so it blows off
questions.explanation: Remove snow and ice from your entire car, including the roof, hood, windows, mirrors, and lights, so you can see and so it does not fly off and endanger others.

questions.prompt: Virginia law requires headlights during rain, fog, snow, or sleet whenever visibility is reduced to:
questions.explanation: Virginia law requires you to use headlights during inclement weather when visibility is reduced to 500 feet, and whenever you use your wipers because of bad weather.

questions.prompt: You must turn your headlights on:
choices: Only when it is fully dark
questions.explanation: You must use headlights from sunset to sunrise. At sunset, turn them on as soon as light begins to fade to make your vehicle more visible.

questions.prompt: Expert drivers try to focus their eyes how far ahead while driving?
choices: At the car directly in front
questions.explanation: Looking ahead helps you spot risks early; expert drivers try to focus their eyes 20 to 30 seconds ahead, about one block in the city.

questions.prompt: When driving in a city or town with street lighting, you should generally use:
questions.explanation: Use low beams when driving in cities and towns, except on streets where there is no lighting.

questions.prompt: The best way to check a vehicle in your blind spot before changing lanes is to:
choices: Rely on your side mirror only
questions.explanation: The best way to see a car in your blind spot is to quickly turn your head and glance over your shoulder before changing lanes or passing.

questions.prompt: A traffic crash must be reported to police and DMV when there is injury, death, or property damage in excess of:
questions.explanation: Officers must forward a written crash report to DMV when a crash results in injury, death, or total property damage in excess of $3,000.

questions.prompt: If you damage an unattended vehicle and cannot find the owner, you must:
choices: Drive away, it is not your fault
Leave a note with your information and report it to police within 24 hours
questions.explanation: Make a reasonable effort to find the owner; if you cannot, leave a note with your name, phone number, the date and time, and a description of the damage, and report the crash to police within 24 hours.

questions.prompt: During a traffic stop at night, in addition to pulling over you should:
choices: Flash your high beams at the officer
questions.explanation: If you are pulled over at night, turn on your vehicle's interior lights so the officer can see inside, and keep your hands in plain view.

questions.prompt: If a police officer asks you to sign a traffic citation, signing it:
choices: Is not an admission of guilt; refusing may lead to arrest
Means you cannot go to court
questions.explanation: Signing a citation is not an admission of guilt; it is a promise to appear. Refusal to sign may result in your arrest.

questions.prompt: After a crash, you should move your vehicle out of the travel lanes when:
choices: It can be moved, no one is injured, and you can do so safely
Only if it is blocking your own driveway
questions.explanation: Drivers must move vehicles from the road immediately if the vehicles can be moved, no one is injured, and the driver can do so safely.

questions.prompt: To pass part two (general knowledge) of the Virginia knowledge exam, you must answer correctly at least:
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the part-two general knowledge questions correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: On part one of the knowledge exam, how many traffic sign questions must you answer correctly to move on to part two?
questions.explanation: You must correctly answer all ten traffic sign questions in part one before you can take part two of the exam.

questions.prompt: If you move, you must notify DMV of your new address within:
questions.explanation: If you move, you are required to notify DMV of your address change within 30 days.

questions.prompt: A driver under 21 convicted of driving after illegally consuming alcohol (BAC at least .02) faces a license suspension of:
questions.explanation: Under Virginia's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 convicted of driving after illegally consuming alcohol loses their driving privilege for one year and faces a minimum $500 fine or 50 hours of community service.

questions.prompt: If you fail the knowledge exam three times, you cannot take it a fourth time until you:
choices: Complete and pass the classroom component of driver education
questions.explanation: If you fail the knowledge exam three times, you cannot take it a fourth time until you complete and pass the classroom component of driver education.

questions.prompt: Under Virginia's implied consent law, operating a vehicle on public roads means you agree to:
choices: Allow your car to be searched anytime
Take a breath or blood test if lawfully arrested for DUI
Waive your right to a lawyer
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, by driving on Virginia's public roads you agree to take a breath or blood test upon request when there is probable cause for a DUI arrest.

questions.prompt: Revocation of your driver's license means your driving privilege is:
choices: Terminated; you must re-apply after the revocation period
questions.explanation: Revocation means your privilege to drive has been terminated. You must re-apply, show proof of legal presence, and pass the exams again after the revocation period passes.

questions.prompt: Virginia law prohibits a driver from holding a cell phone while driving except:
choices: In a driver emergency or when lawfully parked or stopped
When using speakerphone at any time
When stopped at a red light to scroll
questions.explanation: Virginia law prohibits holding a handheld phone or wireless device while driving except in a driver emergency or when the vehicle is lawfully parked or stopped.

questions.prompt: Combining alcohol with another drug, even an over-the-counter medicine, usually:
questions.explanation: Combining alcohol with other drugs usually multiplies the effects of both and can have a disastrous effect on your ability to drive.

questions.prompt: In Virginia, driving under the influence of marijuana is:
choices: Illegal, even though possessing it over age 21 is legal
questions.explanation: Although possession of marijuana over age 21 is legal, driving under the influence of marijuana is still illegal and will affect how you drive.

questions.prompt: An open container of alcohol in the passenger area of your vehicle can lead to a charge if:
choices: The contents are partially removed and you show signs of drinking
It is in a locked trunk
questions.explanation: You may be charged with drinking while driving if there is an open container in the passenger area with contents partially removed and you show signs of drinking.

questions.prompt: Compared with driving sober, your chance of a crash after drinking is about:
questions.explanation: Because alcohol affects judgment and driving ability, your chances of being in a crash are about seven times greater if you drive after drinking than if you drive sober.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the maximum speed limit on unpaved roads in Virginia is:
questions.explanation: Unless a sign states otherwise, the maximum speed limit is 35 MPH for unpaved roads.

questions.prompt: The recommended following distance at 46 to 70 MPH is at least:
questions.explanation: Use a 4-second following distance at 46-70 MPH (2 seconds under 35 MPH, 3 seconds at 35-45 MPH).

questions.prompt: Wet pavement can increase your braking distance by as much as:
questions.explanation: Wet pavement can double your braking distance, so slow down and increase your following distance in the rain.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, if you are driving slower than the traffic around you, you should:
questions.explanation: On a multi-lane highway, stay in the right lane if you are driving slower than surrounding traffic. The left lane is for passing only.

questions.prompt: At a flashing red arrow signal, you must:
choices: Come to a complete stop, yield, then proceed in the arrow's direction when clear
Treat it as a green arrow
questions.explanation: At a flashing red arrow, come to a complete stop, yield to oncoming vehicles and pedestrians, and proceed in the direction of the arrow when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: A steady red arrow pointing right means:
choices: You must stop and may not turn right while it is displayed
You may turn right after stopping
questions.explanation: Virginia law prohibits turns at red arrow lights. A red arrow means you must stop and may not proceed in the direction of the arrow while it is displayed.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow arrow means:
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction if clear, after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians
questions.explanation: At a flashing yellow arrow, you may turn in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear, but you must yield to oncoming vehicles and pedestrians in the intersection.

questions.prompt: At a flashing red light located at a railroad crossing, you must:
choices: Proceed if no gate is down
Come to a complete stop even if you do not see a train
questions.explanation: At a flashing red light you must come to a complete stop; at a railroad crossing you must stop even if you do not see a train.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
questions.explanation: You may not park within 15 feet of a fire hydrant.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of an intersection?
questions.explanation: You may not park within 20 feet of an intersection.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a railroad crossing?
questions.explanation: You may not park within 50 feet of a railroad crossing.

questions.prompt: When you park next to a curb, you may not park more than:
questions.explanation: If you park next to a curb, pull close to it. You may not park more than one foot from the curb.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the center of the road (left)
questions.explanation: Parking downhill with a curb, turn your front wheels toward the curb (to the right) so the vehicle rolls into the curb instead of into traffic.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Away from the curb, toward the road (left)
questions.explanation: Parking uphill with a curb, turn your front wheels away from the curb (to the left) so that if the vehicle rolls back, the rear tire catches the curb.

questions.prompt: A red painted curb generally means:
choices: Stop only to load or unload, stay with your car
Stop only to pick up or drop off passengers
Do not stop, stand, or park
questions.explanation: A red painted curb generally means do not stop, stand, or park. (White: drop off/pick up passengers; Yellow: load or unload, stay with your car.)

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of the entrance to a fire, ambulance, or rescue squad station?
questions.explanation: You may not park within 15 feet of the entrance to a fire, ambulance, or rescue squad station.

questions.prompt: The color used to mark a highway work zone is:
questions.explanation: The color orange marks a work zone and means slow down and be alert.

questions.prompt: If convicted of exceeding the speed limit in a highway work zone, you may be fined up to:
questions.explanation: If you are convicted of exceeding the speed limit in a highway work zone, you may be fined up to $500.

questions.prompt: Using a handheld communications device in a highway work zone carries a fine of:
questions.explanation: If you are convicted of using a handheld communications device in a highway work zone, you will be fined $250.

questions.prompt: At a railroad crossing, school buses are required to:
choices: Stop only when lights are flashing
Always stop, even when the lights are not flashing
Stop only if a train is visible
questions.explanation: School buses must always stop at railroad crossings, even when the lights are not flashing.

questions.prompt: When approaching railroad tracks, you should not start across unless:
choices: You can completely clear the tracks with room on the other side
questions.explanation: Never start across the tracks unless you can clear them completely; make sure there is room for your vehicle on the other side before proceeding.

questions.prompt: Rumble strips placed across travel lanes before a work zone should be:
choices: Driven over slowly, not swerved around
questions.explanation: Rumble strips should be slowly driven over, not swerved around.

questions.prompt: In a work zone, when should you change lanes?
choices: Only after you are completely clear of the work zone
questions.explanation: When driving through a work zone, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and do not change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.
